name: Hill up, Hill down — circular route (PR7)
name_original: Cerro acima, Cerro abaixo — percurso circular (PR7)
region_intro: Family friendly hiking trails around Vaqueiros are situated within the tranquil Serra do Caldeirão mountain range, offering a distinctive landscape of rounded hills shaped by a dense network of seasonal streams. The terrain features diverse areas, from clearings and meadows to rugged sections cut by small water channels. Hikers will encounter Mediterranean flora, including almond, carob, and olive trees, alongside cork oaks and eucalyptus. Elevated points like Cerro da Eira da Capa provide panoramic views of the surrounding hills. Best family friendly hiking trails around Vaqueiros The most popular family friendly hiking route is Hill up, Hill down — circular route (PR7), an 8.1 miles (13.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 41 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the varied topography of the Serra do Caldeirão. Another top favourite among local hikers is In Search of the Enchanted Valley — circular route (PR8 ACT), a moderate 8.6 miles (13.8 km) path. It guides hikers through areas rich in Mediterranean vegetation and offers insights into the region's natural beauty. Local hikers also love the Vaqueiros – Ferrarias loop from Vaqueiros, a 4.7 miles (7.5 km) trail leading through the gentle hills and traditional landscapes of the area, often completed in about 2 hours 7 minutes. Hiking around Vaqueiros is defined by its rounded hills, Mediterranean vegetation, and seasonal watercourses, offering a blend of natural and cultural heritage. The network offers options for different ability levels, with a focus on family friendly hiking trails. The routes in Vaqueiros are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 130 reviews. More than 450 hikers have used komoot to explore Vaqueiros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many family-friendly hiking trails are available around Vaqueiros?
A: There are over a dozen family-friendly hiking trails around Vaqueiros, offering a variety of experiences suitable for all ages. These routes are designed with manageable distances and gentle paths, perfect for getting the whole family outdoors.

Q: What kind of landscapes can we expect on family hikes in Vaqueiros?
A: Family hikes in Vaqueiros traverse the beautiful Serra do Caldeirão, characterized by rounded hills, clearings, meadows, and areas cut by small water channels. You'll see diverse Mediterranean flora, including almond, carob, and olive trees, with cork oaks and eucalyptus also present. In spring, the landscape is vibrant with cistus, gorse, and lavender.

Q: Are there any circular routes suitable for families?
A: Yes, several family-friendly routes are circular, making them convenient for starting and ending in the same spot. A popular option is the &lt;em&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1421572124/hill-up-hill-down-circular-route-pr7" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hill up, Hill down — circular route (PR7)&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/em&gt;, which offers a good moderate challenge for active families.

Q: What are some interesting natural features or landmarks to look out for on these trails?
A: Along the trails, you can discover natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6382163" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeira do Rio Seco&lt;/a&gt;, an intermittent watercourse that can be a refreshing stop when water levels allow. You might also spot ancient cork oaks, which are significant natural monuments in the region. For panoramic views, look for elevated points like Cerro da Eira da Capa.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical sites accessible from the family trails?
A: Absolutely! Vaqueiros is rich in history. You can explore sites related to its Roman copper mining heritage, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4810360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova dos Mouros Mining Park&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features ancient dolmens, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/210220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anta da Masmorra&lt;/a&gt;, offering a glimpse into prehistoric times.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking with family in Vaqueiros?
A: Spring is particularly beautiful, as the landscape bursts with color from various wildflowers. Autumn also offers pleasant temperatures and vibrant foliage. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended if hiking during this season, and always ensure you carry plenty of water.

Q: Are there any trails that are particularly good for birdwatching?
A: The Serra do Caldeirão is known for its rich bird population. Trails that pass through diverse vegetation and near watercourses, even intermittent ones like the Ribeira do Rio Seco, are excellent for birdwatching. The Masmorra Trail (PR8 near Mealha), while not listed in this guide, is regionally known for birdwatching opportunities.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the family-friendly trails in Vaqueiros?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from 138 ratings. Reviewers often praise the tranquil atmosphere, the diverse natural beauty of the Serra do Caldeirão, and the well-maintained paths that make for enjoyable family outings.

Q: Can we find shorter, easier walks for families with younger children?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate, there are easier options. For example, the &lt;em&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32198276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vaqueiros – Ferrarias loop from Vaqueiros&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/em&gt; is a moderate route that is shorter, making it more manageable for families looking for a less strenuous adventure.

Q: Are there any trails that offer a sense of discovery or adventure for kids?
A: The &lt;em&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1422419060/in-search-of-the-enchanted-valley-circular-route-pr8-act" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;In Search of the Enchanted Valley — circular route (PR8 ACT)&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/em&gt; sounds like a perfect adventure for curious young hikers, inviting them to explore the unique landscapes of the region.

Q: Are there places to eat or rest near the family hiking trails?
A: The village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/168371" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vaqueiros&lt;/a&gt; itself serves as a peaceful starting point for many trails and offers local amenities. You might also find small cafes or restaurants in nearby settlements. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/168369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montinho do Lobo&lt;/a&gt; is a restaurant highlighted in the area.
tips: Vaqueiros is a small parish in the municipality of Alcoutim. It is a very peaceful place, where several hiking trails in the region begin. Like other villages in the Algarve, the population of Vaqueiros has reduced significantly over the years, further evidencing a bucolic and peaceful atmosphere.

The small Ribeira da Foupanilha is crossed several times along the PR7 route. Like other watercourses in the Algarve, its volume can be drastically different depending on the season. It is worth paying attention to the fact that, sometimes, in rainy seasons, crossing it can be a somewhat difficult task.

The Cova dos Mouros Mining Park, located in Alcoutim, Portugal, is a theme park and archaeological site. It offers a thematic route that portrays the history of mining and metallurgy from the Chalcolithic period to the beginning of the 20th century. Furthermore, it serves as a reserve for animal species such as ostriches and donkeys, and is located on the former premises of the Cova dos Mouros Mine.
